findings.finding: Operational readiness evidence is lighter than implementation evidence for a desktop app with native and local-AI dependencies.
Automated coverage is strongest in packages/lib, while desktop UI and packaging paths still rely mostly on manual validation.

top_actions: Add a short desktop release/operations note for packaging, native dependency support, and Ollama runtime prerequisites.
Add a small smoke or integration test layer for desktop UI and packaging-critical flows.
Refresh the local audit once CI has real hosted runs and release packaging is documented.

findings.finding: Local APT adoption/profile layer is now present and aligned to canonical doctrine; keep it synchronized with future principle updates.
The working tree already contains modified generated APT publication artifacts. That is acceptable if intentional, but audit/remediation work must not overwrite or normalize them accidentally.

About.tsx still contains a few inline visual comments and decorative gradient/glow details. They use semantic tokens, so this is not a contract violation, but the page is more presentation-heavy than the current APT public-doc posture.
Worker AI/content runtime has clear route grouping and contracts, but the local AI review evidence is scattered across docs, prompt files, and worker code rather than summarized as adoption evidence.

findings.finding: D1 dev and production currently point to the same physical database. The README correctly warns that migrations and writes are production-impacting, but this remains the largest operational risk.
Local APT adoption/profile layer is present, linked to canonical doctrine, and validated in CI.
Full objective design audit now passes with zero contract findings. Manual-review-only areas still require qualitative evidence before Design and Quality can move to Pass.

Main dashboard and daily-system surfaces are dense but task-oriented, using shared design-system components and compact state patterns. No small safe design-gap fix was obvious without a product decision.
Adaptive coaching prompt registry exists and the prompt contract has been expanded, but the prompt remains draft until health-adjacent review and API tests confirm production readiness.
Migration prefix validation currently fails because two migration files use numeric prefix 0013. This predates the docs-only audit follow-up but blocks a clean release gate.

findings.finding: Local APT adoption/profile layer is now present and aligned to canonical doctrine; keep it current as workflow and maturity evolve.
AI prompt source of truth is split between Markdown prompt files and an embedded TypeScript registry. Versions differ for generate-spec (1.1.0 in registry vs 1.0.0 in Markdown), creating drift risk.

Existing full design audit reports 71 major contract findings while still showing overall pass via exception policy. This is valid as a gate result but not strong showcase evidence without a findings summary.
D1 schema documentation still says the current implementation uses mock data/localStorage with D1 as target, while architecture docs say shipped platform uses D1.
Project and refinement flows use shared APT components, states, and explicit filters. No safe local code fix was obvious without changing product behavior.

findings.finding: Workspace root lacks explicit validation scripts used by APT (e.g. check:design, test:contracts, typecheck). Running pnpm run check:design at the repo root failed with: "Missing script: check:design".

findings.finding: Deployment and auth configuration are documented, but this audit did not verify live Cloudflare secrets, JWT issuer/audience values, or D1 environment state.
Automated coverage is centered on the worker package; frontend UI behavior and editorial workflows still rely more on manual validation than repo-level tests.
